question: Two different processes (different BPMN process IDs) each have a message intermediate catch event for `Payment received` with correlation key `order-42`. Both subscriptions are currently open. A single matching message is published. To how many subscriptions is it correlated?

options.text: Both — when subscriptions are opened for different processes, the message is correlated to all of them.
Neither — Zeebe discards the message when more than one subscription matches.
One — only the first deployed process receives the message.
One — only the most recently deployed process version receives the message.

a.text: The docs state directly that "when subscriptions are opened for different processes, the message is correlated to _all_ the subscriptions." The once-per-process rule only collapses subscriptions belonging to the *same* BPMN process ID.

b.text: Multiple matches across different processes are not an error; the message is broadcast to all of them rather than being discarded.

c.text: The docs do not pick winners based on deployment order across different processes; every distinct process with a matching subscription gets the message.

d.text: Deployment version only matters within a single BPMN process ID. Across different process IDs, version ordering is irrelevant and both subscriptions are correlated.

explanation: Message cardinality in Zeebe is keyed by BPMN process ID. Within the same process, a message correlates to only one subscription. Across different processes, the same message is delivered to each one. This lets a single publish fan-out to multiple independent workflows.
